hard.next_step: 6/29 - Zycus submitted our Vendor questions for the RFP - Our response to their RFP is due 6/30 EOD, Downselect by 7/15. Then Demo TBD, Negotiations TBD and Contract award TBD. 6/9 - RFP Released. Responses due 6/24. 6/1 - No material update. RFP coming Mid June. We are shortlisted. 5/15 - Spoke with Elie (VP of Proc). Confirmed updated timeline. As long as pricing is comparable to current Coupa spend, that is fine; looking for best fit, not cheapest. Previous demo good but showed too much; they will provide guidelines for the next demo. Confirmed top 4 finalists. RFP by mid June, business case mid July, decision end of July. 5/13 - Successful 1st demo. 4/14 - Detailed intro call; Coupa replacement referred by Paul Hastings; want to buy this quarter to be ready to dump Coupa upon renewal; AI is big for them; automatically a finalist.
hard.ais_why: Sitecore is a textbook AI-Hungry profile: leadership has embraced AI-driven procurement automation, the team self-built intake automation on Microsoft Forms/Lists/Power Automate plus two Copilot agents against the Coupa API, an SVP has committed to automating AP invoice processing, they asked Zycus to dig into AI invoice scanning and AI-driven ESG risk monitoring, and they run 90%+ Azure infrastructure with a formal RFP in flight.
hard.primary_competitor: Coupa (incumbent being displaced); the live bake-off threat is the three other unnamed RFP finalists

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, engaged RFP finalist replacing Coupa; the 12 Aug close is optimistic against Elie's own end-July decision then Aug-onward negotiation and transition timeline, so the date will slip while the deal stays strong.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 Apr 2026, ~88 days in Shortlisted. Close 12 Aug 2026 = 37 days out (pushed from 30 Jul on 30 Jun). Last buyer touch 1 Jul (Harsh acknowledged the RFP response, promised feedback and next steps). RFP response submitted 30 Jun; downselect by 15 Jul; then demo, negotiation and contract award all TBD. Elie's stated path (15 May): decision end July, then negotiation plus transition Aug 2026 to Mar 2027, so signed contract by 12 Aug is roughly 2-4 weeks ahead of his own plan. Timing-only risk; the deal is live and progressing.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case / Upside (decision end July, contract award August) rather than a 12 Aug Commit; the finance budget approver is unmapped and contract award falls after the current close date.
evidence: Elie Sauma, 15 May 2026 call: 'I would like to be able to present back a business case in mid July latest... get a decision by July and then... August time frame till March next year to kind of work on transition.'
Next Step (SF): '6/29 - Zycus submitted our Vendor questions for the RFP - Our response to their RFP is due 6/30 EOD, Downselect by 7/15. Then Demo TBD, Negotiations TBD and Contract award TBD.'
Task 1 Jul 2026, Harsh Sejpal (inbound email): 'Thank you for sharing the proposal. We have received it... We will review it internally and get back to you soon with feedback and next steps.'

metrics.narrative: The pain is vivid but never quantified in dollars. Elie Sauma said Coupa's Invoice Smash 'has just not worked' and his team manually data-enters invoices, that procurement approves every purchase 'no matter the amount' (significant overhead), and that one-year software renewals are 'a lot of work for my team with very little impact' (Avoma 30 Apr and 15 May 2026). On the 30 Apr demo Ansen Malanakarot confirmed 90%+ PO-backed invoices data-entered by country-specific AP mailboxes, and spend is 'in the hundreds of millions' with no spend-under-management figure tracked. No cost, cycle-time, headcount or savings number was ever put on the table, so the value case is qualitative today. Quantifying AP-automation and tail-spend savings against current Coupa spend is the ranked move before the mid-July business case.
sources: Avoma 1st Demo 30 Apr 2026 (Elie Sauma, Ansen Malanakarot)

economic_buyer.narrative: Elie Sauma (VP, Head of Procurement & Travel, SF Primary Decision Maker) controls the procurement decision and explicitly owns the commercial framing: 'I would like to be able to present back a business case in mid July' (15 May) and 'that's how our CFO wants it' on one-year renewals, showing final budget authority sits with an unnamed CFO / finance function he must sell. An SVP has also committed to automating AP invoice processing (AIS field), a second finance-side sponsor. Budget is constrained by PE-owned cost discipline ('the budget limitation that we have right now with sidequarters', 30 Apr). The named finance/CFO approver has never been engaged and receives the case second-hand, so multi-threading to them before the end-July decision is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Elie Sauma = Decision Maker, Primary)

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are explicit and buyer-stated. Elie set the overarching rule on 15 May: 'I'm not looking for the cheaper solution... I'm gonna go with the best tool possible within my budget' with pricing 'comparable to current spend with Coupa'. The functional must-haves surfaced on the 30 Apr demo: AI-powered AP invoice extraction and no-PO-no-pay handling (Ansen's priority), Microsoft Teams / Copilot-native intake with a simplified phased rollout ('probably we'll start there'), eInvoicing to government portals (Peppol) for Belgium, France and Singapore entities, native CLM (their Coupa CLM was never adopted), and AI-driven ESG/supplier-risk monitoring. These are being formalized in the RFP, whose responses were due 30 Jun and are scored to a 15 Jul downselect.
sources: Avoma 1st Demo 30 Apr 2026 (Elie Sauma, Ansen Malanakarot)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal RFP bake-off that the buyer is running to a clear clock. Read from the sequence: intro call 9 Apr, 1st demo 30 Apr (Elie, Harsh, Ansen), a 15 May check-in where Elie confirmed a down-select to four finalists, RFP released 9 Jun, Zycus vendor questions 16 Jun, signed NDA and FAQ exchanged, Zycus response submitted 30 Jun, downselect by 15 Jul, then finalist demo, negotiation and contract award (all TBD). Elie's plan: decision end July, transition Aug 2026 to Mar 2027 to be off Coupa by renewal. Procurement (Elie/Harsh) runs sourcing; a mid-July business case goes to finance/CFO for budget sign-off. Elie has been on medical leave since 9 Jun with Harsh Sejpal covering, which slows champion-level decisions.
sources: Task 9 Jun 2026 (Elie Sauma auto-reply: medical leave from 6/9)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early but visible. An NDA was executed in June (Steve sent the signed NDA 9 Jun; Sitecore returned a countersigned NDA plus FAQ on 24 Jun), and Sitecore is a Microsoft/SharePoint shop that stores RFP documents in SharePoint. Sitecore requires vendors to sign a Sitecore MSA to provide services (Elie, 30 Apr: 'they have to use Sitecore MSA'), signalling buyer-paper preference for the eventual contract. No MSA/SOW redlines, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement portal or signatory chain have surfaced yet; the compelling event is getting off Coupa by its renewal via the Aug-Mar transition window. Getting the contracting path and buyer-paper requirements in writing after the 15 Jul downselect would create visibility.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and buyer-articulated, owned by Elie Sauma and the AP team. Coupa is barely used: 'Coupa's only used basically for financial approval of a requisition... The CLM was never fully implemented... Invoice Smash we have extensively tried to make work, and it's not working' (Elie, 30 Apr). Their homegrown Microsoft Forms/Lists intake is unoperationalized, AP invoices are manually keyed from country mailboxes with no upfront duplicate/fraud check (Ansen, 30 Apr), and one-year renewals drain the team. Post-Thoma-Bravo Coupa has been 'difficult for the last two, three years' and would not credit back the failed module. Inferred pain: eInvoicing compliance exposure across EU/APAC entities and untracked tail spend under PE cost pressure.
sources: Avoma 1st Demo 30 Apr 2026 (Elie Sauma, Ansen Malanakarot)

champion.narrative: Elie Sauma (VP, Head of Procurement & Travel) is a strong, transparent champion who brought the deal in via a trusted Paul Hastings CPO reference (his mentor), told Zycus 'you guys have everything we need' and 'I would be very surprised if we don't include you in the RFP' (15 May), coaches on the process, and volunteered to shape the finalist demo guidelines. His advocacy is genuine but two things temper it: he is procurement, not the finance/CFO budget owner, so the deal is single-threaded to him, and he has been on medical leave since 9 Jun with Harsh Sejpal as the working contact. Converting his advocacy into direct access to the finance approver and locking the AP SVP sponsor is the open champion work.

competition.narrative: This is a Coupa displacement, not a Coupa defence: the incumbent has failed on AP (Invoice Smash), was never adopted for CLM, and has been 'difficult' since the Thoma Bravo buyout, and Sitecore wants to be off it by renewal. The live threat is the RFP bake-off itself: three other unnamed finalists scored against Zycus to the 15 Jul downselect (Elie, 15 May: 'down select to probably 4 vendors'). Relich, a Coupa-integrated risk bolt-on Sitecore once eyed, is dormant. The wedge is Elie's stated rule, best fit within the current Coupa budget, not cheapest: win on unified Merlin AI (AP Smartdesk, Teams-native intake, autonomous negotiation), proven Coupa-to-Zycus migration via Paul Hastings, and eInvoicing/ESG coverage, then embed that value in the mid-July business case so a lower-priced rival cannot undercut on price alone.

deal_movement.summary: Live and progressing through a formal RFP. In the last 30 days the RFP was released (9 Jun), Zycus submitted vendor questions (16 Jun), NDA and FAQ were exchanged (24 Jun), Zycus submitted its RFP response (30 Jun), Harsh acknowledged receipt (1 Jul), and a downselect is set for 15 Jul. The close date was pushed 30 Jul to 12 Aug. Champion Elie Sauma has been on medical leave since 9 Jun with Harsh covering.

items.change: Zycus submitted its RFP response; Harsh Sejpal acknowledged receipt and promised feedback and next steps
Close date pushed from 30 Jul to 12 Aug (Steve Ovadje)
Sitecore returned a countersigned NDA plus FAQ document and set the proposal deadline of 30 Jun
RFP released to shortlisted vendors, responses due 24 Jun (later moved to 30 Jun), downselect by 15 Jul
Elie Sauma began medical leave; Harsh Sejpal became the working point of contact
Forecast category moved Best Case -> Upside Key Deal (Michael McCarthy)
Elie confirmed Zycus as a finalist of four and set the decision timeline (business case mid-July, decision end July)

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is the three other unnamed RFP finalists, live in a bake-off scored to the 15 Jul downselect (Elie, 15 May 2026). Coupa is the incumbent being actively displaced (failed Invoice Smash, unadopted CLM, post-Thoma-Bravo decline) and now serves mainly as the budget benchmark rather than a defender, so it is a low current threat. Relich, a Coupa-integrated risk bolt-on eyed on the 9 Apr call, is dormant. Zycus is a confirmed finalist with a trusted Paul Hastings reference and a best-fit-not-cheapest selection bias in its favour.

competitors.name: Unknown RFP finalists (3 others)
competitors.quote: We have one more vendor to meet next week... down select to probably 4 vendors. (Elie Sauma, 15 May 2026)
competitors.source: Avoma call 15 May 2026 (Elie Sauma); SF Next_Step__c
competitors.how_we_win: Win the buyer's stated criteria in the finalist demo and RFP: unified Merlin AI across AP/intake/CLM/sourcing (not point solutions), Teams-native intake and Azure fit, proven Coupa-to-Zycus migration (Paul Hastings), and best-fit-within-budget value baked into the mid-July business case so no rival undercuts on price alone.

competitors.name: Coupa (incumbent)
competitors.quote: Invoice Smash we have extensively tried to make work, and it's not working... once they got acquired by Thoma Bravo... they've been difficult for the last two, three years. (Elie Sauma, 9 Apr / 30 Apr 2026)
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 9 Apr and 1st Demo 30 Apr 2026 (Elie Sauma); SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa is being replaced, not defended. Demonstrate working AP automation (AP Smartdesk vs failed Invoice Smash), a native single-data-core Merlin platform vs Coupa's bolt-on acquisitions, and active AI innovation; match current Coupa budget and de-risk the migration before renewal.

competitors.name: Relich
competitors.quote: We were looking at Relich, which integrates easily with Coupa... that is a part that we know is kind of a gap right now. (Elie Sauma, 9 Apr 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Position native Zycus supplier-risk / ESG monitoring (anti-slavery, anti-bribery, compliance) as removing the need for a Relich-style Coupa bolt-on.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the two things the buyer cares most about, AI-driven AP invoicing and Microsoft Teams-native intake, with real exposure on price-to-Coupa-budget and a preference for a simple, phased rollout.

items.criterion: AI-powered AP invoice extraction and no-PO-no-pay handling
items.quote: What's very important to us is the AI and the invoicing component... I'd rather spend time on that side than the contracting. (Elie Sauma)
The automation that Harsh and I built leveraging Microsoft Form and Microsoft List... connected to Coupa via API. (Elie Sauma)

items.criterion: eInvoicing to government portals (Peppol) for Belgium, France, Singapore entities
items.quote: They're implementing eInvoicing through the Peppol network... Does Zycus also have the capabilities to link up to those eInvoicing government portals? (Ansen Malanakarot) - Yes, we do. (Zycus)

items.criterion: Pricing comparable to current Coupa spend (best fit, not cheapest)
items.quote: As long as pricing is comparable to current spend with Coupa, that's fine... we'll have to build in whatever transition cost... that's the part that needs to be part of the total TCO. (Elie Sauma)

items.criterion: Simple, phased implementation starting with core use cases
items.quote: It's probably gonna be kind of phased in... a lot of the requests that come, all they need is the PO... can you just simplify this? (Elie Sauma)

items.requirement: Deliver a tighter finalist demo focused only on the buyer's priority use cases per their forthcoming RFP guidelines
items.quote: I would have preferred that you guys just focused on the stuff we wanted... the RFP will have some instruction to how we want the demo to look like.

items.requirement: Confirm AP Smartdesk can auto-reject a no-PO invoice and email the supplier that it lacks a PO number
items.quote: We have a no PO, no pay policy... can the system automatically turn around an email telling the supplier this invoice has not been accepted because it's lacking a PO number?

items.deliverable: Send Elie the change-management and implementation-planning resources for the transformation
items.grounding_quote: You did have some thoughts about the change management as well as planning for this whole process and transformation. So if you did want those resources, we can send you those offline.

items.deliverable: Confirm and share sample of the AP Smartdesk no-PO auto-rejection email to the supplier
items.grounding_quote: Whether the communication goes back to the supplier, I will confirm that before I say yes... I'll still share a sample there.

items.deliverable: Provide the RFP demo guidelines specifying the priority use cases for the finalist demo
items.grounding_quote: The RFP will have some instruction to how we want the demo to look like and what they should be focusing on... it makes it easier for the participant.

items.deliverable: Return the RFP scoring feedback and downselect decision with next steps
items.grounding_quote: We will review it internally and get back to you soon with feedback and next steps.

items.quote: I would like to be able to present back a business case in mid July... that's how our CFO wants it.

items.area: Quantified ROI / savings figures for the business case
items.quote: I'm not looking to save money, I'm just looking to replace Coupa... within the budget we are today.

items.area: Identity and positioning of the three other RFP finalists

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: break the single-thread to the finance approver before the end-July decision, win the 15 Jul downselect with a guideline-tight finalist demo, and neutralize the three unknown finalists on value not price.
flags: Multi-thread past Elie to the finance/CFO budget approver and lock the AP SVP sponsor before the mid-July business case; the deal is single-threaded to Elie, who is on medical leave from 9 Jun.
Win the 15 Jul downselect by tailoring the finalist demo strictly to the buyer's RFP guidelines on AP AI, eInvoicing (Peppol) and Teams intake; they flagged the first demo showed too much.
Neutralize the three unnamed finalists by anchoring the case to best-fit-within-current-Coupa-budget plus a proven Paul Hastings migration reference, so a cheaper rival cannot undercut on price.

items.name: Elie Sauma
items.title: Vice President - Head of Procurement & Travel
items.sentiment: Positive, advocating; SF Primary Decision Maker who brought the deal in via a Paul Hastings reference
items.risk: Also the SF Decision Maker but not the finance budget owner; deal is single-threaded to him and he is on medical leave from 9 Jun.
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker, Primary); Avoma calls 9 Apr, 30 Apr, 15 May 2026

items.name: Harsh Sejpal
items.title: Procurement Analyst - Global Procurement
items.sentiment: Engaged; running the RFP logistics and the working contact while Elie is on leave
items.risk: Process owner, not a decision-maker; keep him close to preserve momentum during Elie's absence.

items.name: Ansen Malanakarot
items.title: Business System Analyst
items.sentiment: Positive; owns AP/finance-systems and eInvoicing requirements (NetSuite ERP, Peppol)
items.risk: Technical evaluator on AP automation and integration; his fit assessment feeds the RFP score.

champion_strength.summary: Elie Sauma is an active, transparent champion who sourced the deal via a trusted Paul Hastings CPO reference, confirmed Zycus as a finalist, and offered to shape the finalist demo guidelines. Strength is tempered by two facts: he is procurement not finance, so the deal is single-threaded to him, and he has been on medical leave since 9 Jun with Harsh Sejpal covering. Converting his advocacy into direct access to the finance approver is the open work.
champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 9 Apr, 30 Apr, 15 May 2026 (Elie Sauma); SF Next_Step__c

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strongly positioned: the AI story is buyer-pulled, not seller-pushed. Sitecore self-built Copilot agents on the Coupa API and asked Zycus specifically for AI invoice scanning, Teams-native Merlin intake, autonomous negotiation for tail spend, and AI ESG risk monitoring, all of which Merlin covers on a single data core. The task is to keep the finalist demo tightly on these AP/intake AI use cases.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 9.0 / Strong is corroborated by the calls: leadership embraced AI automation, the team built two Copilot agents, an SVP committed to AP automation, and they explicitly requested AI invoice extraction and AI ESG monitoring. The field is accurate, not over-rated.

items.detail: The finance/CFO budget approver who receives the mid-July business case has never been engaged; deal is single-threaded to Elie.
PE-owned cost discipline caps price at current Coupa spend, and transition cost must fit inside the TCO Elie presents to finance ('sidequarters' budget limitation).
items.source: Avoma 1st Demo 30 Apr and call 15 May 2026 (Elie Sauma)

items.detail: Champion Elie Sauma on medical leave from 9 Jun; momentum depends on Harsh Sejpal until he returns.
Three unnamed RFP finalists are scored against Zycus to the 15 Jul downselect; their positioning is unknown.
Close date pushed 30 Jul to 12 Aug; decision is end-July with negotiation and contract award still TBD after the downselect, so 12 Aug is optimistic.

confidence_signals.summary: Live and engaged: RFP response submitted 30 Jun, buyer acknowledged 1 Jul and promised feedback, downselect 15 Jul. Not cooling; the only soft spot is the champion's medical leave.
items: RFP response submitted 30 Jun; Harsh acknowledged receipt 1 Jul with a promise of feedback and next steps.
Buyer named concrete dates (downselect 15 Jul, decision end July) and is running a structured RFP.
Champion Elie Sauma on medical leave from 9 Jun, but Harsh Sejpal is keeping the process moving.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're a finalist-of-four Coupa replacement with a strong procurement champion (Elie Sauma) and a 9/10 AI fit; the deal turns on winning the 15 Jul downselect and getting a finance approver we've never met to fund a TCO that must stay within the current Coupa budget.

win_position.text: Earns roughly 66 at Shortlisted (which caps confidence near 70): the incumbent Coupa is actively being displaced after a failed AP module, Zycus is a confirmed finalist of four with a 9/10 AI fit and a trusted Paul Hastings reference (Elie Sauma, 15 May 2026).

Held below the stage cap because the finance/CFO budget approver who receives the mid-July business case has never been engaged and the deal is single-threaded to Elie, who has been on medical leave since 9 Jun.
Three unnamed RFP finalists are scored against Zycus at the 15 Jul downselect, and price is capped at current Coupa spend, so transition cost is the swing factor in the TCO.

deal_momentum.text: RFP released 9 Jun, Zycus submitted its response 30 Jun, and Harsh acknowledged receipt 1 Jul with a downselect due 15 Jul (SF activities).
Elie named concrete dates on 15 May: business case mid-July, decision end July, transition Aug 2026 to Mar 2027 to be off Coupa by renewal.
Close date was pushed 30 Jul to 12 Aug on 30 Jun, and demo, negotiation and contract award are all still TBD after the downselect.

customer_commitment.text: Elie stated best-fit-within-budget not cheapest: 'I'm gonna go with the best tool possible within my budget' (15 May), and called Zycus a near-certain finalist.
Buyer executed the NDA and is running a formal RFP they invited Zycus into, with AI invoicing named as the priority to spend time on (30 Apr).
Commitment is procurement-level only; the CFO/finance approver has not seen or funded the business case, and budget sits under PE-owned cost discipline.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded to Elie Sauma, the champion and SF Decision Maker, who is on medical leave from 9 Jun with Harsh Sejpal covering.
Budget ceiling is current Coupa spend and transition cost must fit the TCO Elie presents to finance mid-July (15 May).
Three unknown finalists remain live in the RFP bake-off through the 15 Jul downselect.

executive_alignment.evidence: Finance/CFO budget approver never engaged; single-threaded to procurement (Elie, 15 May 2026).

timeline.evidence: Close pushed 30 Jul to 12 Aug; decision end-July with negotiation and award still TBD after the 15 Jul downselect.

critical_signals.text: Coupa is the incumbent being displaced after a failed AP module and post-buyout decline, and Zycus is one of four finalists, but three rivals are still unnamed and scored against us at the 15 Jul downselect.
On the 15 May call Elie confirmed Zycus as a near-certain finalist, said we 'have everything they need', and set the rule that he will pick the best tool within budget, not the cheapest.
Price must stay within current Coupa spend and the transition cost is the swing factor in the total cost Elie presents to finance in mid-July.
Harsh Sejpal is now the active point of contact while Elie is on medical leave from 9 Jun, and the finance budget approver has still never been met.
Ansen raised eInvoicing to government portals (Peppol) for the Belgium, France and Singapore entities as a must-have alongside AI AP automation.

items.action: Book a post-submission RFP debrief with Harsh to confirm downselect criteria and Zycus standing.
items.trigger: Harsh acknowledged the RFP response and promised feedback and next steps; downselect due 15 Jul.
items.expected_effect: Locks Zycus into the finalist round and surfaces gaps before scoring closes.

items.action: Secure Elie's RFP demo guidelines and stage a tight AP-AI and eInvoicing finalist demo.
items.trigger: Elie said the RFP would carry instructions on what the demo should focus on after the first demo showed too much.
items.expected_effect: Wins the finalist round on the buyer's own priority use cases and avoids the over-showing feedback.

items.action: Co-author the mid-July business case with Elie framing TCO within current Coupa budget including transition cost.
items.trigger: Elie plans to present a business case mid-July and flagged transition cost as the part he must justify.
items.expected_effect: Arms the champion for the finance sign-off and blocks a price-only comparison with rivals.

items.action: Ask Elie to introduce the finance/CFO budget approver ahead of the end-July decision.
items.trigger: Elie owns budget authority only up to a CFO who has never engaged; business case goes to finance mid-July.
items.expected_effect: Breaks the single-thread and de-risks the budget approval; if no reply in 10 business days, escalate via the deal owner's manager.

items.action: Deliver a Coupa-to-Zycus migration and transition plan anchored to the Paul Hastings reference.
items.trigger: Elie wants to be off Coupa by renewal over an Aug 2026-Mar 2027 transition and trusts the Paul Hastings CPO.
items.expected_effect: De-risks the switching cost that Elie must fold into TCO and reinforces the trusted reference.

customer_preference.evidence: Elie: 'I think that you guys have everything we need... I would be very surprised if we don't include you in the RFP' and best-fit-within-budget not cheapest (15 May); positive but still one of four in a live RFP.

business_case.evidence: Vivid qualitative pain (failed Invoice Smash, manual AP, one-year-renewal overhead, untracked tail spend) but no ROI/savings figures; Elie explicitly wants to stay within current Coupa budget, not save money.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay suite (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake)
scope_change.to: Full Source-to-Pay suite unchanged; buyer signals a phased rollout starting with AP invoicing and Teams intake
scope_change.detail: Products__c and the $180k amount are unchanged, but Elie wants a simplified, phased implementation ('probably we'll start there'), a phasing signal to watch rather than a formal scope reduction.

expansion_context.note: Net-new Coupa replacement, not an expansion; no prior Closed-Won on Sitecore, so executive/finance access is not inherited and must be earned via the RFP and business case.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120d; field history to Apr 2026
gaps: Finance/CFO budget approver is unnamed; only the role is known (Elie presents the business case to them mid-July).
The three other RFP finalists are unnamed; their positioning and pricing are unknown until the 15 Jul downselect.
No quantified ROI/savings numbers have been articulated by the buyer.
